- 9:00–10:00 (Richard)

- 9:00–9:30 (Kathleen)
- 10:30–11:00 (Kathleen)
- 11:00–11:30 (Sarah)
- 12:00–12:30 (Kathleen)
- 13:30–14:00 (Gloria)
- 13:30–15:30 (Kathleen, overlaps Gloria)
- 14:00–14:30 (Sarah)
- 14:30–15:00 (Gloria)
- 15:30–16:00 (Gloria)
- 16:00–16:30 (Kathleen)

The merged busy periods are:
- 9:00–12:30
- 13:30–15:30 (includes 13:30–14:00, 14:00–14:30, 14:30–15:00)
- 15:30–16:00 (Gloria)
- 16:00–16:30 (Kathleen)

Thus, the free intervals are:
- 12:30–13:30
- 16:00 (only if exactly 16:00, but 16:00–16:30 is busy for Kathleen, and 15:30–16:00 is busy for Gloria, so no gap at 16:00)
- 16:30–17:00

After 14:30, the free intervals are:
- 16:30–17:00 (30 minutes)

Gloria prefers after 14:30, and 16:30–17:00 is the only free slot after 14:30 that is 30 minutes long and available for all.

Let me clarify: if Jose is busy from 9:30 to 12:00, that means he is busy until 12:00, and then from 13:00 to 17:00, he is busy starting at 13:00. So, what about the time between 12:00 and 13:00? Typically, if a meeting ends at 12:00, the time 12:00-13:00 should be free unless otherwise specified. Similarly, if a meeting starts at 13:00, the time just before 13:00 is free. So, Jose should be free from 12:00 to 13:00.

But in the Observation 2, it says "Tuesday 12:00-13:00 is not available", which contradicts my reasoning. Perhaps the tool interprets the busy periods as inclusive of the end times? Let me re-read the input.

- Busy from 9:30 to 12:00: free after 12:00.
- Busy from 13:00 to 17:00: free before 13:00.

So, Jose should be free from 12:00 to 13:00.

Similarly, Barbara is free during 12:00-13:00 because her busy periods are "10:00 to 10:30, 15:30 to 16:00, 16:30 to 17:00" on Tuesday, so she is free at 12:00-13:00.

Therefore, the slot 12:00-13:00 should be available. The tool might have a bug or I might have misused it.
